title: Skill: capability-tier abstraction and orchestration-evolution principles
description:
Philosophy (from the human, encode it prominently): the ticks skill must stay HIGH-LEVEL enough that it always takes best advantage of the latest Claude orchestration capabilities and models. Speak in capabilities and tiers, never hardcoded model names or harness specifics; the orchestrator resolves to the best available at runtime. When the harness offers a better primitive than the skill describes, prefer the primitive and note the gap.

Changes, all in skills/ticks/ (claude-runner.md primarily, SKILL.md one short principle mention):

1. Add the philosophy as a short principles note near the top of claude-runner.md (Mental model section area).
2. Rewrite "Choosing a model per tick": replace the haiku/sonnet/opus table with CAPABILITY TIERS — fastest/cheapest tier (1-2 files, complete spec, mechanical), balanced default tier (multi-file integration), most-capable tier (design judgment, broad codebase understanding, ambiguous specs). State that tier->model mapping is resolved at run time from what the harness offers; any concrete names are a dated example at most. Add the REVIEWER-TIER RULE: review with a model at least as capable as the one that wrote the code; epic final reviews default to the most capable tier.
3. Implementer prompt template: add standard boilerplate as the first instruction block — if the run base branch (from the epic base_branch field) is not the branch the worktree was created from, merge it first (git merge <base-branch> --no-edit) and verify a named artifact from the prior wave exists; report BLOCKED if not. Parameterize with <base-branch>/<artifact> placeholders like the rest of the template.
4. Epic-close retro, promotion table: add a sixth destination row — "Learning about the orchestration PROCESS itself (harness behavior, worktree/branching gotchas, prompt patterns)" -> "the ticks skill text (claude-runner.md / tick-patterns.md), so it travels to every repo; repo learnings.md keeps only repo-specific entries". One sentence after the table on why (per-repo learnings do not transfer; process knowledge must live in the skill).
5. Closing the epic / completion report: document merge-not-squash rationale (tick-stamped commits are the audit trail retros mine); require the completion report to state the epic branch integration status (merged / awaiting PR / awaiting human); recommend a PR + CI gate as the default for repos with CI, configured via .tick/config.md Rules.
6. Reviewing the work: add the FOUNDATION-REVIEW principle (when a wave-1 tick defines a contract consumed by multiple dependents, review it before launching the dependent wave — cheapest highest-leverage review). Add the severity policy: blocker = fix before close-out; should-fix = fix or explicitly accept in the retro report; nit = fix opportunistically.

Keep each addition terse and in the voice of the existing text. Do not renumber/restructure unrelated sections.
acceptance_criteria: claude-runner.md: tier-based model section + reviewer rule; template base-branch boilerplate; promotion table has skill-text row; merge/PR guidance; foundation-review + severity policy. SKILL.md: one philosophy mention. No contradictions introduced.
